prompt: I want to build a todo app with ralph
assertions: Output mentions 'greenfield'
Does NOT ask about resuming existing spec
Asks the first Goal dimension question

prompt: Set up ralph for this existing project
assertions: Output mentions 'brownfield'
Reads existing package.json or source files before interviewing
Asks whether to resume or start over
Does NOT silently overwrite existing files

prompt: I want to build a chat app. / The primary user is developers. / It uses React and Supabase.
assertions: After each answer, shows [Goal ✓/∙∙/✗] [Constraints ✓/∙∙/✗] [Criteria ✓/∙∙/✗] status
Targets the weakest dimension next
Asks ONE question at a time, not multiple
Response contains exactly ONE question mark (or one question block)
Does NOT list multiple questions to answer at once

prompt: I want to build a weather dashboard using the OpenWeatherMap API
assertions: During constraint phase, invokes or mentions /api-integration-checklist
Records CORS/proxy decision
Every task object in tasks.json has a 'description' field
No description is empty string or null
Descriptions contain enough context for an independent worker
Task 1 (setup) has no depends_on
Subsequent tasks have depends_on: [1] at minimum
Wave display matches the depends_on graph
Worker recommendation equals max tasks in any single wave

setup: Interview for project with multiple parallel UI tasks
assertions: Tasks in the same wave that could touch overlapping files have isolated: true
isolated flag is NOT set on sequential tasks with no overlap
Prints '[OK] .ralph/SPEC.md written' etc.
Prints 'ralph team N' as instruction for the user
Does NOT execute ralph team automatically

setup: UI project with multiple E2E scenarios
assertions: No single 'write all E2E tests' task exists
E2E scenarios appear in individual task descriptions
SPEC.md E2E section maps scenarios to task IDs

prompt: Clone the landing page at https://example.com using ralph
assertions: Checks for agent-browser installation (which agent-browser)
Prompts user to install if missing (npm install -g @anthropic-ai/agent-browser)
Launches agent-browser to analyze the reference URL
Task descriptions include /ui-reverse-engineering

setup: Directory with CLAUDE.md containing project-specific constraints
assertions: Reads CLAUDE.md before interviewing
Incorporates existing constraints from CLAUDE.md into the interview

prompt: I want to build a todo app
assertions: Output contains 'Project type: greenfield' or 'Project type: brownfield'
Output contains announcement of structured interview or dimension tracking intro
Asks for explicit confirmation before overwriting existing spec
Does NOT silently delete .ralph/SPEC.md

prompt: I want to build a todo app. / stuff
setup: Empty directory, user gives vague 1-word answer to first goal question
assertions: Asks a follow-up Goal question to clarify the vague answer
Does NOT jump to tech stack or Constraints questions

prompt: I want to use React with Next.js
setup: Interview in progress, user specifies stack
assertions: Mentions or invokes vercel-react-best-practices skill
Locks data fetching pattern in SPEC.md
Presents at least 2 options with pros and cons
Includes a recommendation among the options

setup: Interview in Criteria phase, user gives only 1 vague criterion
assertions: Does NOT mark Criteria dimension as complete
Pushes for more specific and testable done criteria

prompt: User logs in and sees dashboard
setup: Interview in Criteria phase, user describes only 1 flow
assertions: Asks for more E2E scenarios
Criteria dimension stays partial, not complete
Pushes back with something like 'How would you test that?'
Does NOT accept the vague criterion as-is
Targets Criteria (the weakest dimension) next
Does NOT target Constraints before addressing the empty Criteria
Shows warning that Constraints and/or Criteria are still incomplete
Asks to proceed anyway rather than silently continuing
Displays architecture summary box
Asks 'Does this accurately capture what we decided?' or similar confirmation
Waits for user approval before proceeding

setup: Stack already decided as React + Supabase in Phase 1
assertions: Does NOT present new A/B/C options for stack choices
Only confirms existing choices already made
Displays spec and task list in prescribed format
Asks for approval before writing files
Does NOT write .ralph/SPEC.md or tasks.json before approval

prompt: Change task 3 to depend on task 2
setup: Phase 4 plan displayed, user requests modification
assertions: Revises the plan with the requested change
Re-displays the updated plan
Asks for approval again
SPEC.md contains ## What section
SPEC.md contains ## What NOT section
SPEC.md contains ## Architecture section
SPEC.md contains ## Tech Stack section
SPEC.md contains ## Done section
SPEC.md contains ## E2E Test Scenarios section
SPEC.md contains ## External Dependencies section
CLAUDE.md contains How to Work section
CLAUDE.md contains Testing section
CLAUDE.md contains Code Correctness Rules section
CLAUDE.md contains Definition of Done section
CLAUDE.md contains Convergence Condition section

prompt: I want user auth with social login
assertions: Task is split into multiple sub-tasks (e.g., data model, core logic, API/UI)
Does NOT create one monolithic task for the entire feature
Task 1 is 'Project setup' or equivalent with no depends_on
All other tasks have depends_on that includes 1

prompt: Clone this site, it has lots of animations: https://example-animated.com
assertions: Animation-specific tasks include /transition-reverse-engineering in description
Also includes /ui-reverse-engineering for layout tasks (both skills used, not just one)

prompt: Clone the landing page at https://example.com
assertions: SPEC.md contains ## Reference section
Reference section includes the provided URL https://example.com
Output contains 'tmux attach -t ralph-' instruction
Output contains 'ralph status --watch' instruction
TypeScript is included in tech stack by default
Does NOT default to plain JavaScript

setup: No testing preference specified by user
assertions: Vitest is included as the unit testing framework
Playwright is included as the E2E testing framework

setup: User mentions a reference site during any phase
assertions: Checks for agent-browser availability
Uses agent-browser for site analysis, not MCP Playwright
